archetypes.name: 🗺️ All Journey Archetypes Browse all journeys

keyMyths.title: Enoch's Ascension
keyMyths.description: Enoch, a righteous man, walked with God and was taken directly to heaven without experiencing death. He became a celestial being, often associated with divine knowledge and scribal duties.

keyMyths.title: The Ascension of Elijah
keyMyths.description: The prophet Elijah was carried to heaven in a whirlwind, riding in a chariot of fire. His successor, Elisha, witnessed the event and received a double portion of Elijah's spirit.

keyMyths.title: Ganymede's Abduction
keyMyths.description: Ganymede, a Trojan prince known for his beauty, was abducted by Zeus (or an eagle sent by Zeus) to serve as cupbearer to the gods on Mount Olympus. This ascent elevated him to a position of divine service and immortality.

keyMyths.title: Heracles' Apotheosis
keyMyths.description: After completing his Twelve Labors and enduring a painful death, Heracles (Hercules) ascended to Mount Olympus and was granted immortality. He became a god, marrying Hebe and joining the Olympian pantheon.

keyMyths.title: The Ascension of Jesus
keyMyths.description: Following his resurrection, Jesus ascended to heaven in the presence of his apostles. This event marked the end of his earthly ministry and his return to the Father.

sources.name: The Holy Bible
sources.description: A collection of sacred texts containing accounts of Enoch, Elijah, and Jesus' ascensions.
sources.title: The Holy Bible

sources.name: Homer, Iliad
sources.description: An ancient Greek epic poem featuring the abduction of Ganymede.
sources.title: Homer, Iliad

sources.name: Pseudo-Apollodorus, Bibliotheca
sources.description: A comprehensive compendium of Greek myths, detailing Heracles' apotheosis.
sources.title: Pseudo-Apollodorus, Bibliotheca

sources.name: Mircea Eliade, Shamanism: Archaic Techniques of Ecstasy
sources.description: A seminal work exploring shamanic practices and the concept of spiritual ascents.
sources.title: Mircea Eliade, Shamanism: Archaic Techniques of Ecstasy

sources.name: Wendy Doniger, The Implied Spider: Politics and Theology in Myth
sources.description: A comparative mythology study discussing the theological implications of myths, including those involving ascensions.
sources.title: Wendy Doniger, The Implied Spider: Politics and Theology in Myth
